items.title: Sam Kerr fires Australia to victory over the Philippines in Asian Cup opener

items.title: Queensland talent scouts use 'world-first' AI to broaden search for 2032 Olympians
items.summary: A sliding doors moment can be all it takes to put someone on the path to elite sport. But those in charge of finding our next Olympians have a "world-first" idea to change that.

items.title: Labor promises to wipe public school fees from 2027 while Liberals set target for zero litter in oceans within a decade
items.summary: Labor has vowed to abolish public school fees for primary and high school students from 2027, if it's returned to Government, while the Liberal Party said it will commit $4.3 million to stop stormwater litter reaching South Australia's coastline.

items.title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's death may not lead to regime change in Iran
items.summary: The death of Iran's supreme leader leaves a power vacuum in a perilously unstable country. It could end with a military takeover and a new leader with a no-limits approach to war.

items.summary: An independent review into oil and gas company Inpex has found widespread and persistent failures in how emissions were calculated, recorded and reported, dating back to the start of production in 2018.
